pending.body: `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-430` explicitly accepts contract-marked prompt overrides that omit `## Nearby work`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340` also treats that legacy shape as valid. But `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:182-190` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:366-367` still inject guidance that says `Nearby work` is an authoritative canonical section whenever the marker is present. On a supported legacy override, prompt-review/completion/final-review agents are told to preserve and use a section that does not exist, which can create false contract-drift or scope complaints.

`src/cli/project.rs:2586-2606` never truncates `NearbyBeadEntry.title` (and even falls back to the title for `scope_summary`), while `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:92-111` can only get back under budget by dropping whole entries and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:140-148` renders the full title verbatim. A single long bead title can therefore blow the nearby-work byte budget or cause the highest-priority direct dependency to be removed entirely instead of being shortened, violating the per-entry and section-budget requirements.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
